channel.title: SCF Convergence on PJ's Personal Website
channel.description: Recent content in SCF Convergence on PJ's Personal Website

item.title: DIIS: Python code for H2O using minimal STO-3G basis set
item.description: The theory for the DIIS method can be found in the Notes section, or directly click here. As a reference, a normal SCF procedure (without DIIS implementation) took around 38 iterations as compared to just 11 iterations with DIIS implementation (we will see in this example). The energy and density convergence criteria were same in both cases. Some one-electron, and two-electron integrals are required for the code to work and those files are made available here.

item.title: DIIS: A method to accelarate SCF convergence
item.description: Introduction Direct Inversion of the Iterative Subspace (DIIS) is a method first proposed by Pulay1 which accelarates the convergence of a normal SCF procedure. Method Iterative procedures can give us a set of trial vectors {$\mathbf{p}^i$}. Thus we can form a residual (error) term between two sucessive iterations. $$\mathbf{e}^i = \mathbf{p}^{i+1} - \mathbf{p}^i$$ The DIIS method assumes that the final solution can be extrapolated as the linear combination of the current subspace generated from previous iterations.
